symbols.name: Tool
symbols.signature: export type Tool<Input extends AnyObject = AnyObject, Output = unknown, P extends ToolProgressData = ToolProgressData> = { name: string; call(...): Promise<ToolResult<Output>>; description(...): Promise<string>; checkPermissions(...): Promise<PermissionResult>; prompt(...): Promise<string>; ... }
symbols.purpose: Core structural contract every tool must implement, including execution, permissions, rendering, and classification hooks.

symbols.name: ToolUseContext
symbols.signature: export type ToolUseContext = { options: { commands, tools, mcpClients, ... }; abortController: AbortController; getAppState(): AppState; setAppState(...): void; messages: Message[]; ... }
symbols.purpose: Ambient context bag threaded through every tool call and query loop iteration, carrying tools, state accessors, abort signal, and session metadata.

symbols.name: ToolPermissionContext
symbols.signature: export type ToolPermissionContext = DeepImmutable<{ mode: PermissionMode; alwaysAllowRules: ToolPermissionRulesBySource; alwaysDenyRules: ToolPermissionRulesBySource; ... }>
symbols.purpose: Immutable snapshot of permission state consulted on every tool-use decision; threaded from AppState into ToolUseContext.

symbols.name: buildTool
symbols.signature: export function buildTool<D extends AnyToolDef>(def: D): BuiltTool<D>
symbols.purpose: Factory that fills in safe fail-closed defaults (isEnabled=true, isConcurrencySafe=false, isReadOnly=false, checkPermissions=allow) so individual tool modules only declare what differs.

symbols.name: Tools
symbols.signature: export type Tools = readonly Tool[]
symbols.purpose: Nominal collection type for tool arrays, enabling precise tracking of where tool sets are assembled and passed.

symbols.name: ToolResult
symbols.signature: export type ToolResult<T> = { data: T; newMessages?: ...; contextModifier?: (context: ToolUseContext) => ToolUseContext; mcpMeta?: ... }
symbols.purpose: Return value from tool.call(), optionally injecting new messages or mutating the ToolUseContext for the next iteration.

symbols.name: QueryEngine
symbols.signature: export class QueryEngine { constructor(config: QueryEngineConfig); async *submitMessage(prompt: string | ContentBlockParam[], options?): AsyncGenerator<SDKMessage, void, unknown> }
symbols.purpose: Stateful per-conversation engine for the headless/SDK path; owns message history, file cache, and usage tracking across turns, delegating each turn to query().

symbols.name: QueryEngineConfig
symbols.signature: export type QueryEngineConfig = { cwd: string; tools: Tools; commands: Command[]; mcpClients: MCPServerConnection[]; canUseTool: CanUseToolFn; getAppState: () => AppState; setAppState: ...; ... }
symbols.purpose: Configuration bundle passed once to QueryEngine constructor; carries tools, commands, state accessors, model overrides, and budget constraints.

symbols.name: query
symbols.signature: export async function* query(params: QueryParams): AsyncGenerator<StreamEvent | RequestStartEvent | Message | TombstoneMessage | ToolUseSummaryMessage, Terminal>
symbols.purpose: Top-level async generator driving the model–tool loop: calls the API, executes tools, applies compaction/snip/collapse, and yields stream events until a terminal condition.

symbols.name: QueryParams
symbols.signature: export type QueryParams = { messages: Message[]; systemPrompt: SystemPrompt; userContext: {...}; systemContext: {...}; canUseTool: CanUseToolFn; toolUseContext: ToolUseContext; maxTurns?: number; taskBudget?: {...}; ... }
symbols.purpose: Immutable input bundle for a single query() invocation; not mutated across loop iterations (mutable state is carried in queryLoop's local State).

symbols.name: queryLoop
symbols.signature: async function* queryLoop(params: QueryParams, consumedCommandUuids: string[]): AsyncGenerator<..., Terminal>
symbols.purpose: Private infinite loop handling one model call per iteration, including context preparation, streaming, tool dispatch, compaction, and transition decisions.

symbols.name: State (query loop)
symbols.signature: type State = { messages: Message[]; toolUseContext: ToolUseContext; autoCompactTracking: ...; maxOutputTokensRecoveryCount: number; turnCount: number; transition: Continue | undefined; ... }
symbols.purpose: Mutable cross-iteration snapshot destructured at the top of each loop body and reassigned atomically at continue sites.

symbols.name: Command
symbols.signature: export type { Command, CommandBase, LocalCommandResult, PromptCommand, ... } from './types/command.js'
symbols.purpose: Re-exported union type representing built-in slash commands; consumed by tools and QueryEngine options to handle /compact, /clear, /resume, etc.

symbols.name: getCommands / COMMANDS
symbols.signature: const COMMANDS = memoize((): Command[] => [ addDir, advisor, agents, ..., vita, ... ])
symbols.purpose: Memoized factory assembling the full slash-command registry; not executed until first call to avoid reading config at module init time.

symbols.name: getSlashCommandToolSkills
symbols.signature: export const getSlashCommandToolSkills = memoize((cwd: string) => ...)
symbols.purpose: Loads user-defined skill files from the project's .claude/skills directory and wraps them as prompt-type Commands.

symbols.name: addToHistory / removeLastFromHistory
symbols.signature: export function addToHistory(command: HistoryEntry | string): void
symbols.purpose: Manages the persistent per-session command history file used for up-arrow REPL navigation, with pasted-content deduplication via a hash store.

symbols.name: setup
symbols.signature: export async function setup(cwd, permissionMode, allowDangerouslySkipPermissions, worktreeEnabled, worktreeName, tmuxEnabled, customSessionId?, worktreePRNumber?, messagingSocketPath?): Promise<void>
symbols.purpose: Bootstrap sequence run once at session start: sets cwd, captures hooks config, initializes UDS messaging, creates worktree if requested, and wires terminal backup restoration.

symbols.name: State (bootstrap)
symbols.signature: type State = { originalCwd: string; projectRoot: string; totalCostUSD: number; sessionId: SessionId; registeredHooks: ...; invokedSkills: Map<...>; isRemoteMode: boolean; ... }
symbols.purpose: Process-global singleton holding immutable session identity (session ID, project root, start time) and mutable session-lifetime counters (cost, API duration, model usage).

symbols.name: AppState
symbols.signature: export type AppState = DeepImmutable<{ settings: SettingsJson; toolPermissionContext: ToolPermissionContext; tasks: { [taskId: string]: TaskState }; mcp: { clients, tools, commands, resources }; plugins: ...; agentDefinitions: ...; ... }>
symbols.purpose: React/Zustand-style immutable UI+session state tree covering permission context, running tasks, MCP connections, plugin state, speculation state, and bridge connectivity.

symbols.name: AppStateStore
symbols.signature: export type AppStateStore = Store<AppState>
symbols.purpose: Type alias for the generic Store<AppState>; the single source of truth for reactive UI state, accessed via getState/setState/subscribe.

symbols.name: Store
symbols.signature: export type Store<T> = { getState: () => T; setState: (updater: (prev: T) => T) => void; subscribe: (listener: Listener) => () => void }
symbols.purpose: Minimal reactive store primitive (identity-check, notify-all pattern) backing AppState; no external dependency on Redux/Zustand.

symbols.name: AppStateProvider
symbols.signature: export function AppStateProvider({ children, initialState, onChangeAppState }): React.ReactNode
symbols.purpose: React context provider wrapping the Store; enforces single-provider invariant and wires optional onChange callback for SDK consumers.

symbols.name: TaskState
symbols.signature: export type TaskState = LocalShellTaskState | LocalAgentTaskState | RemoteAgentTaskState | InProcessTeammateTaskState | LocalWorkflowTaskState | MonitorMcpTaskState | DreamTaskState
symbols.purpose: Discriminated union of all background task variants stored in AppState.tasks; enables type-safe dispatch in task managers.

symbols.name: Task
symbols.signature: export type Task = { name: string; type: TaskType; kill(taskId: string, setAppState: SetAppState): Promise<void> }
symbols.purpose: Minimal polymorphic dispatch interface for task managers; only kill() is called polymorphically — spawn and render are type-specific.

symbols.name: LocalAgentTaskState
symbols.signature: export type LocalAgentTaskState = TaskStateBase & { type: 'local_agent'; agentId: string; prompt: string; agentType: string; messages?: Message[]; isBackgrounded: boolean; retain: boolean; ... }
symbols.purpose: State for in-process agent tasks (AgentTool subagents); tracks message stream, backgrounding, disk-load status, and eviction deadline.

symbols.name: RemoteAgentTaskState
symbols.signature: export type RemoteAgentTaskState = TaskStateBase & { type: 'remote_agent'; sessionId: string; log: SDKMessage[]; todoList: TodoList; ultraplanPhase?: ...; ... }
symbols.purpose: State for remote CCR-hosted agent tasks (ultraplan, ultrareview, autofix-pr); polled via SSE and displayed in the tasks panel.

symbols.name: RemoteSessionManager
symbols.signature: export class RemoteSessionManager { constructor(config: RemoteSessionConfig, callbacks: RemoteSessionCallbacks); connect(): Promise<void>; disconnect(): void; sendMessage(content): Promise<void>; respondToPermission(...): Promise<void> }
symbols.purpose: Manages the WebSocket subscription and HTTP POST channel for a single remote CCR session, including permission request/response flow.

symbols.name: ReplBridgeHandle / BridgeCoreHandle
symbols.signature: export type ReplBridgeHandle = { send(msg): void; stop(): void; ... }; export type BridgeCoreHandle = ReplBridgeHandle & { state: BridgeState; ... }
symbols.purpose: Handle returned by the always-on bridge (replBridge), allowing the REPL to send messages to and disconnect from the claude.ai session gateway.

symbols.name: runBridgeHeadless
symbols.signature: export async function runBridgeHeadless(opts: HeadlessBridgeOpts, signal: AbortSignal): Promise<void>
symbols.purpose: Non-interactive daemon-worker entrypoint for the remote-control bridge; no TUI, auth via IPC, logs to stdout pipe, throws on fatal errors.

sessionId, cost accumulators, model usage counters

patterns.name: Dual-State Plane: Process-Global vs React-Reactive
patterns.description: Two distinct state layers coexist. bootstrap/state.ts holds a module-level singleton for session identity, cost counters, and telemetry — read by any module via direct getter calls. AppStateStore holds React-reactive UI state (tasks, permissions, MCP connections) via a minimal pub-sub Store<AppState>. The two planes are intentionally decoupled: bootstrap state never subscribes to React; React state never reads bootstrap state directly.
evidence: bootstrap/state.ts:31 (// DO NOT ADD MORE STATE HERE)

patterns.name: Async Generator Streaming Pipeline
patterns.description: The entire query lifecycle is expressed as a chain of async generators: queryLoop yields StreamEvents, query() wraps it with command lifecycle notifications, QueryEngine.submitMessage() wraps that with session transcript writes and SDK message shaping. Each layer can intercept, transform, or withhold events (e.g., withholding prompt-too-long errors until recovery is confirmed) without breaking the generator contract.

patterns.name: buildTool Factory with Fail-Closed Defaults
patterns.description: All ~60 tools are defined via buildTool(), which applies fail-closed defaults: isConcurrencySafe=false, isReadOnly=false, checkPermissions=allow (defer to general permission system), toAutoClassifierInput='' (skip classifier). Individual tools override only what they need. This prevents new tools from accidentally bypassing permission checks or parallel execution guards.

patterns.name: Feature-Gated Dead Code Elimination via bun:bundle
patterns.description: Optional subsystems (COORDINATOR_MODE, HISTORY_SNIP, CONTEXT_COLLAPSE, REACTIVE_COMPACT, VOICE_MODE, BRIDGE_MODE, KAIROS, etc.) are loaded via conditional require() guarded by feature() from 'bun:bundle'. At build time, the bundler eliminates false branches and their string literals, producing a smaller external build without ifdef preprocessing. This pattern appears at least 15 times across query.ts and QueryEngine.ts alone.

patterns.name: ToolUseContext as Ambient Dependency Injection
patterns.description: Rather than passing individual dependencies (getAppState, abortController, setInProgressToolUseIDs, etc.) to each tool call, all ambient dependencies are bundled into a ToolUseContext object threaded from QueryEngine through every layer. Tools receive this context and call only what they need. The contextModifier field in ToolResult allows tools to mutate the context for subsequent iterations without breaking concurrency safety.

patterns.name: Task Union Pattern for Heterogeneous Concurrency
patterns.description: Background concurrency is modelled as a discriminated union of TaskState variants (local_agent, remote_agent, in_process_teammate, local_bash, dream, etc.) stored in AppState.tasks. The polymorphic Task interface only exposes kill() — spawn and render are type-specific. This gives the UI a single subscription point while keeping task logic isolated in per-type modules.

patterns.name: Prompt Cache Preservation via Immutable Message Mutation Discipline
patterns.description: The query loop is careful never to mutate API-bound messages in place. When backfillObservableInput needs to add derived fields for SDK observers, it clones the message and the affected block before yielding, leaving the original untouched. Comments explicitly cite prompt caching (byte mismatch) as the reason. Similarly, sticky-on latches in bootstrap/state.ts (afkModeHeaderLatched, fastModeHeaderLatched, cacheEditingHeaderLatched) prevent mid-session feature toggles from busting the server-side prompt cache.

surprises: buildTool's default checkPermissions returns { behavior: 'allow' } — permission enforcement is opt-in per tool, not opt-out; security relies on the general permission system in permissions.ts running before tool.checkPermissions

The query loop can withhold error messages from downstream consumers (prompt-too-long, max_output_tokens) to allow internal recovery loops to retry before SDK callers see any error — a silent retry abstraction spanning multiple generator layers
bootstrap/state.ts explicitly warns 'DO NOT ADD MORE STATE HERE — BE JUDICIOUS WITH GLOBAL STATE' and 'THINK THRICE BEFORE MODIFYING', suggesting historical over-growth of the module

AppState.tasks is explicitly excluded from DeepImmutable because TaskState variants contain function types (abortController, callbacks) — the tasks map is structurally mutable inside an otherwise immutable tree

Feature flags use 'bun:bundle' import (not a runtime flag system) so the bundler performs static dead-code elimination at build time, meaning feature() calls cannot be evaluated at runtime — they must appear in if/ternary positions only

ToolUseContext carries two separate setAppState paths: setAppState (no-op for async subagents to prevent state leakage) and setAppStateForTasks (always reaches root store for session-scoped infrastructure), a subtle dual-write design to handle nested agent isolation

The session prompt cache is protected by sticky boolean latches in bootstrap/state (e.g. afkModeHeaderLatched) that flip true on first activation and never reset — ensuring mid-session GrowthBook/settings toggles cannot change request headers and bust a multi-hundred-thousand-token cached prompt

Commands registry (COMMANDS in commands.ts) is memoized and intentionally not initialized at module load time to avoid reading config files during import — a common pattern to keep module evaluation side-effect-free
